segments.text: Lesson 9.

translations.zh-Hans: 第9课。

segments.text: Cats.

translations.zh-Hans: 会飞的猫。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: How do cats try to protect themselves when falling from great heights?

translations.zh-Hans: 猫从高处坠落时怎样设法保护自己？

segments.text: Cats never fail to fascinate human beings.

translations.zh-Hans: 猫总能使人着迷。

segments.text: They can be friendly and affectionate towards humans, but they lead mysterious lives of their own as well.

translations.zh-Hans: 它们可以对人友好而亲昵，却也过着属于自己的神秘生活。

segments.text: They never become submissive like dogs and horses.

translations.zh-Hans: 它们从不像狗和马那样顺从。

segments.text: As a result, humans have learned to respect feline independence.

translations.zh-Hans: 因此，人类学会了尊重猫的独立性。

segments.text: Most cats remain suspicious of humans all their lives.

translations.zh-Hans: 大多数猫终其一生都对人保持戒心。

segments.text: One of the things that fascinates us most about cats is the popular belief that they have nine lives.

translations.zh-Hans: 猫最令我们着迷的事情之一，是人们普遍相信它有九条命。

segments.text: Apparently, there is a good deal of truth in this idea.

translations.zh-Hans: 显然，这种说法确有不少道理。

segments.text: A cat's ability to survive falls is based on fact.

translations.zh-Hans: 猫从坠落中幸存的能力是有事实依据的。

segments.text: the New York Animal Medical Center made a study of 132 cats over a period of five months.

translations.zh-Hans: 纽约动物医疗中心曾在五个月内研究了132只猫。

segments.text: All these cats had one experience in common.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些猫都有一段共同经历。

segments.text: They had fallen off high buildings, yet only eight of them died from shock or injuries.

translations.zh-Hans: 它们都曾从高楼坠落，却只有8只因休克或受伤而死亡。

segments.text: Of course, New York is the ideal place for such an interesting study, because there is no shortage of tall buildings.

translations.zh-Hans: 当然，纽约是进行这项有趣研究的理想场所，因为那里从不缺高楼大厦。

segments.text: There are plenty of high-rise windowsills to fall from.

translations.zh-Hans: 可供猫跌落的高层窗台多得很。

segments.text: One cat, Sabrina, fell 32 stories, yet only suffered from a broken tooth.

translations.zh-Hans: 一只名叫萨布丽娜的猫从32层楼坠下，却只摔断了一颗牙。

segments.text: Cats behave like well-trained paratroopers, a doctor said.

translations.zh-Hans: “猫表现得像训练有素的跳伞员，”一位医生说。

segments.text: It seems that the further cats fall, the less they are likely to injure themselves.

translations.zh-Hans: 猫似乎坠落得越远，受伤的可能性反而越小。

segments.text: In a long drop, they reach speeds of 60 miles an hour and more.

translations.zh-Hans: 长距离坠落时，它们的速度会达到每小时60英里甚至更快。

segments.text: At high speeds, falling cats have time to relax.

translations.zh-Hans: 高速坠落时，猫有时间放松身体。

segments.text: They stretch out their legs like flying squirrels.

translations.zh-Hans: 它们像鼯鼠一样伸展开四肢。

segments.text: This increases their air resistance and reduces the shock of impact when they hit the ground.

translations.zh-Hans: 这会增大空气阻力，并减轻落地时撞击造成的冲击。
